examples.title: Algorithm: Process Scheduling
examples.description: Demonstrates a basic process scheduling algorithm
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ScheduleProcesses
INPUT: processes (list of processes)
OUTPUT: scheduledProcesses (list of scheduled processes)

BEGIN
 WHILE processes is not empty DO
 currentProcess = GetNextProcess(processes)
 IF currentProcess is not null THEN
 ExecuteProcess(currentProcess)
 AddToScheduledProcesses(currentProcess)
 END IF
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates a basic process scheduling algorithm. It iterates through a list of processes, executes each one, and adds it to a list of scheduled processes.

examples.title: Python: Process Management
examples.description: Demonstrates process creation and management using Python's multiprocessing module
examples.code:
import multiprocessing
import time

def worker(num):
 print(f'Worker {num} started')
 time.sleep(2)
 print(f'Worker {num} finished')

if __name__ == '__main__':
 processes = []
 for i in range(5):
 p = multiprocessing.Process(target=worker, args=(i,))
 processes.append(p)
 p.start()

 for p in processes:
 p.join()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates process creation and management using the multiprocessing module. It creates five worker processes, starts them, and waits for them to finish.

examples.title: C: Memory Management
examples.description: Demonstrates memory allocation and deallocation using C's malloc and free functions
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates memory allocation and deallocation using the malloc and free functions. It allocates memory for an integer, assigns a value to it, and then frees the memory.
